threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the Tube' instead of 'the Overground' when you’re waiting at Hackney Central station. It’s a dead giveaway, mate.
Last night on the 333, a bloke serenaded the whole bus with a ukulele rendition of 'Wonderwall'. Oddly, it made the 2 AM crawl feel less grim.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I noticed the street musicians setting up at Hermannplatz before dawn, weaving their own rhythms into the city's heartbeat.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a club that serves currywurst at 4 AM while you’re dancing to techno. It's the perfect late-night fuel.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re from here if they know to avoid BART when it rains—soggy commuters act like it’s the first drizzle of the year.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week a guy was juggling on the 14 Mission, but it was the lady holding a 10-pound bag of dog food who stole the show.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: You'd think all the locals flock to the park, but we actually prefer the hidden plazas. Less noise, more chances to catch up without the crowds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll find them staring at the metro map like it's a treasure map, trying to decode which line leads to 'La Sagrada'.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'The Tube' instead of just 'London transport' like we all do. It's like wearing a new badge no one else recognizes.
Last week on the 59, a guy serenaded the whole bus with old soul tracks. A few joined in, but the driver just rolled his eyes and kept driving.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy in a full suit arguing with a street musician over a beat drop at the Rathaus Neukölln. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find spontaneous techno parties in the middle of a park thanks to the Berlinale's after-parties, mixing art and chaos like nowhere else.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from the Mission when they can name all the taco trucks and tell you which one has the best lengua that's open past midnight.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 bus packed with people on their phones, but the lady next to me was reading a physical book. Felt so out of place in 2023.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often miss how late we eat; dinner at 7 PM is practically breakfast in Gràcia. Restaurant staff look at you like you’re lost.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their confused faces near the 'café con leche' area; they still ask for a 'latte' instead of embracing the local lingo.
